title: Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides
intro: Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides, also known as Microsorium, is a type of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family, native to tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal supplements and tonics.
taxonomy: Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Lepidomicrosorium, which is a part of the family Polypodiaceae. Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides is a type of epiphytic fern native to Asia. This species is further classified as a member of the phylum Pteridophyta and kingdom Plantae. Lepidomicrosorium microsorioides is also known as the 'Microsorium-like' fern due to its similar characteristics to the genus Microsorium.
